content:
## 🇮🇳 India

### Fintech Unicorn Moneyview Gets SEBI Go-Ahead for ₹1,500 Crore IPO

Lending platform Moneyview secured SEBI's final approval on July 3 to launch its ₹1,500 crore IPO, marking a watershed moment for Indian fintech unicorns moving toward public markets. The company plans to deploy ₹650 crore from fresh issuance toward loan disbursals under default loss guarantee arrangements with NBFC partners, addressing a critical gap in underserved lending. The offering combines fresh equity with offer-for-sale from existing shareholders, signaling institutional confidence in the personal credit space after years of fintech consolidation.

**Why it matters.** For an AI engineer building fintech tools or considering roles in India's lending ecosystem, Moneyview's path to public markets changes the liquidity calculus entirely. Until now, most Indian fintech unicorns either exited to acquirers or stayed private. This IPO proves investor appetite for consumer lending platforms; it validates the model for underserved borrowers and softens the venture capital constraints that plagued early-stage fintech hiring. Teams building AI for credit decisioning, fraud detection, or loan origination now have a visible exit ramp and can poach talent from newly-liquid shareholders. The ₹650 crore DLG deployment also signals regulatory comfort with tech-enabled lending post-RBI guidelines, making policy friendlier for AI-driven risk models.

**Action.** Track Moneyview's DRHP filing details on the SEBI website once the red herring prospectus is filed post-approval; it will disclose exact loan book composition and AI stack. Follow the IPO timeline closely—this becomes the template for other Indian fintech unicorns like Slice, Razorpay, and PhonePe evaluating their own public market debuts. Pull the company's loan performance metrics to understand how tech-enabled lending at scale has performed through the 2024-26 credit cycle, critical context if you're building risk models.

*Sources: [[Fintech Unicorn Moneyview Gets SEBI Approval For ₹1,500 Cr+ IPO](https://inc42.com/buzz/fintech-unicorn-moneyview-gets-sebi-approval-for-%E2%82%B91500-cr-ipo/)]([Fintech Unicorn Moneyview Gets SEBI Approval For ₹1,500 Cr+ IPO](https://inc42.com/buzz/fintech-unicorn-moneyview-gets-sebi-approval-for-%E2%82%B91500-cr-ipo/)) · [[Moneyview Gets SEBI Nod for ₹1,500 Crore IPO Launch](https://www.5paisa.com/news/moneyview-sebi-approval-1500-crore-ipo-public-listing-plans)]([Moneyview Gets SEBI Nod for ₹1,500 Crore IPO Launch](https://www.5paisa.com/news/moneyview-sebi-approval-1500-crore-ipo-public-listing-plans)) · [[Moneyview & Chandan Steel Get SEBI's Nod To Launch IPO](https://ipocentral.in/latest-ipo-approval-3-july-moneyview-ipo-approval/)]([Moneyview & Chandan Steel Get SEBI's Nod To Launch IPO](https://ipocentral.in/latest-ipo-approval-3-july-moneyview-ipo-approval/))*

---

### Indian AI Startups Hit $676 Million in H1 2026, Sarvam Becomes Second Unicorn

Indian AI startups exploded to a ₹5,645 crore ($676 million) funding run in the first six months of 2026—quadrupling the $162 million raised across H1 2025—across 57 deals, even as overall Indian startup funding declined 9 percent to $5.2 billion. The surge culminated June 15 when Sarvam AI closed a $234 million Series B at a $1.5 billion valuation, becoming India's second AI unicorn after Freshworks, with HCLTech leading the round at ₹1,250 crore ($150 million). The IndiaAI Mission, backed by ₹10,372 crore in government commitments, influenced 66 percent of institutional capital allocators' India-focused AI bets, effectively lowering entry costs for sovereign compute and indigenous model builders.

**Why it matters.** This is the inflection point for an AI engineer in Hyderabad or Bengaluru deciding whether to stay independent, join a funded team, or apply to roles at HCLTech, TCS, and other large IT services firms now building AI applications on sovereign infra. Sarvam's valuation and HCLTech's lead prove enterprises will back Indian-language foundation models and vertical AI targeting healthcare, finance, defense, and agriculture—not just wrapper applications. Capital concentration away from generic chatbots toward models with defensible moats means your AI role matters more to investors if it's embedded in domain logic or runs on IndiaAI's 38,000-GPU cluster. ₹10,372 crore of government backing de-risks the compute bet; the ecosystem now resembles China's sovereign AI push circa 2020, and India's first true AI unicorns are being born not from venture whims but state policy.

**Action.** File a bookmark for the AIKosh portal (9,500+ datasets, 273 sectoral models)—these are free compute and models available to startups and employees; cost of experimentation just dropped. Track HCLTech's AI subsidiary announcements and hiring—they're betting corporate IT will be AI-first by 2027 and will acquire or incubate teams now. Pull Sarvam AI's open-source Sarvam-30B and Sarvam-105B models from Hugging Face and audit their performance on Indian-language benchmarks; if these match or beat GPT-4o on Hindi, Telugu, Kannada, the training bar for domestic models just reset.

*Sources: [[Indian AI Startup Funding Soars Over 4X YoY In H1 2026](https://inc42.com/features/indian-ai-startup-funding-soars-over-4x-yoy-in-h1-2026-but-is-it-enough-to-compete-globally/)]([Indian AI Startup Funding Soars Over 4X YoY In H1 2026](https://inc42.com/features/indian-ai-startup-funding-soars-over-4x-yoy-in-h1-2026-but-is-it-enough-to-compete-globally/)) · [[India's AI Capital Surge: $3.94B in Q1 and a New Unicorn](https://aitechconnect.in/news/india-ai-funding-surge-neysa-sarvam-q1-2026)]([India's AI Capital Surge: $3.94B in Q1 and a New Unicorn](https://aitechconnect.in/news/india-ai-funding-surge-neysa-sarvam-q1-2026)) · [[India AI & Deep Tech Investment Report 2025](https://www.ventureintelligence.com/india-ai-deeptech-investments-landscape-report-2026)]([India AI & Deep Tech Investment Report 2025](https://www.ventureintelligence.com/india-ai-deeptech-investments-landscape-report-2026))*

## 💹 Global Stocks

### SHUTTERSTOCK (NASDAQ): -28.0% as $3.7B Getty Images merger collapses on UK regulatory veto

Shutterstock plummeted 28.0% after the company's proposed $3.7 billion merger with Getty Images collapsed due to a U.K. regulatory obstacle. Getty Images' board unanimously decided to terminate the merger agreement by July 6, 2026, ending months of negotiations. The deal had faced increasing antitrust scrutiny from UK authorities over concerns about market concentration in stock imagery licensing. Shutterstock shares tumbled to reflect the lost strategic opportunity, while Getty Images declined approximately 6.0%.

**Why it matters.** The collapse signals renewed regulatory assertiveness toward major technology and media consolidation in Europe, particularly around AI training data and digital asset licensing. For investors tracking M&A sentiment in 2026, this reversal suggests buyers can no longer assume regulatory approval paths are clear, even for asset-light tech combinations. The decision specifically reflects the UK regulator's push-back on consolidation in digital content licensing, a sector increasingly critical as AI companies compete for proprietary training material.

**Action.** Watch Shutterstock's Form 8-K (expected July 6 filing) for details on merger termination fees and management commentary. Monitor Getty Images' next earnings call for strategic pivot toward alternative partnerships or standalone positioning. Key resistance level at $45; support emerging around $38 on capitulation selling.

*Sources: [[CNBC earnings report](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/30/nike-nke-q4-2026-earnings.html)]([CNBC earnings report](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/30/nike-nke-q4-2026-earnings.html)) · [[SEC filings](https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK=SSTI&type=8-K&dateb=&owner=exclude&count=100)]([SEC filings](https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&CIK=SSTI&type=8-K&dateb=&owner=exclude&count=100)) · [[UK regulatory news](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/01/meta-stock-cloud-ai-compute.html)]([UK regulatory news](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/01/meta-stock-cloud-ai-compute.html))*

### NIKE (NYSE): -8.0% despite earnings beat on Greater China sales collapse

Nike shares fell 8.0% in extended trading after reporting fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings of $0.20 adjusted per share—well ahead of $0.13 expected—and revenue of $10.97 billion against $10.86 billion consensus. The stock's decline reflected singular weakness: Greater China sales contracted 12.0%, signaling persistent demand softness in a region that represents roughly 15.0% of Nike's business. CEO Elliott Hill acknowledged the setback, saying "the results aren't there yet," particularly in Nike sportswear and Jordan streetwear categories where sell-through remains challenged.

**Why it matters.** Nike's China stumble exposes the fragility of consumer spending in the world's second-largest economy, even as gross margins recovered 8.9% (buoyed by a $986 million tariff refund). For investors watching multinational consumer discretionary exposure, the headline miss on print offset gains elsewhere; the message: supply-chain tailwinds and operational efficiency cannot paper over geography-specific demand destruction. This China read will carry weight into Q2 FY2027 guidance, which management reiterated as "flattish" through the first half.

**Action.** Review Nike's 10-Q for segment-level commentary on Greater China recovery trajectory and competitive positioning against local peers like Li-Ning and Anta. Watch Elliott Hill's earnings call (transcript due by July 5) for specific market-share actions and Asia expansion plans. Technical support: $73.50; resistance near $82.00.

*Sources: [[Nike Q4 FY2026 earnings](https://wwd.com/footwear-news/shoe-industry-news/nike-nke-q3-2026-earnings-china-declines-1238694938/)]([Nike Q4 FY2026 earnings](https://wwd.com/footwear-news/shoe-industry-news/nike-nke-q3-2026-earnings-china-declines-1238694938/)) · [[SGI Europe earnings analysis](https://www.sgieurope.com/financial-results/nike-q3-beats-estimates-but-stock-falls-on-china-outlook/120352.article)]([SGI Europe earnings analysis](https://www.sgieurope.com/financial-results/nike-q3-beats-estimates-but-stock-falls-on-china-outlook/120352.article)) · [[Fortune CEO turnaround analysis](https://fortune.com/2026/07/04/nike-earnings-elliott-hill-nascent-comeback/)]([Fortune CEO turnaround analysis](https://fortune.com/2026/07/04/nike-earnings-elliott-hill-nascent-comeback/))*
